name: MicroLaunch
description: Launch directory for micro-SaaS and indie products with a month-long ranking window, giving smaller products an extended chance to climb the leaderboard among makers and early adopters.

auth.notes: Email/password account (social sign-in may be available) to submit and schedule a launch. Free listings join the monthly leaderboard; paid tiers add featured slots and badges.

best_practices: MicroLaunch is built for micro-SaaS and indie products — embrace the small, focused niche rather than over-pitching scale.
Ranking runs over a month, so plan a sustained push: schedule for early in the month and keep nudging your audience across the window.
Update your listing during the month (new features, milestones) to stay fresh and keep collecting upvotes over the long window.
Write a tagline that names the narrow problem and the exact user; this crowd rewards sharp positioning over broad claims.
Be transparent about pricing — indie buyers here appreciate clear, honest, often affordable plans.
Use a clean square logo and a couple of screenshots so a small product still looks polished on the leaderboard.
Engage with comments and feedback throughout the month, not just on day one, since the window stays open.

questions.label: Tagline
questions.help: Name the narrow problem and the exact user.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: What the micro-SaaS does, the niche it serves and who it's for.

questions.label: Pricing
questions.help: Be clear and honest; this audience values transparent pricing.

questions.label: Launch date
questions.help: Schedule early in the month to maximise the ranking window.
